Regions Artists Timeline All works

Vincent van Gogh

4 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
The Potato Eaters
by Vincent van Gogh
Early Van Gogh · 1885 · Nuenen
Dark, muddy, deliberately ugly. Van Gogh wrote that he wanted to show hands that had dug the earth eating the potatoes those hands had grown. His breakthrough, though critics hated it.
Sunflowers
by Vincent van Gogh
Post-Impressionism · 1888
Painted in Arles to decorate Gauguin's guest bedroom. Van Gogh made seven versions; in letters, he called them an ode to gratitude.
Café Terrace at Night
by Vincent van Gogh
Post-Impressionism · 1888 · Arles
The yellow terrace still stands in Arles (renamed after the painting). No black in the night sky — deep cobalt blue everywhere instead. Van Gogh's first major work done at night.
The Starry Night
by Vincent van Gogh
Post-Impressionism · Van Gogh · 1889
Painted from the window of his asylum room at Saint-Rémy, from memory. The village below is imagined; the swirling sky he said he could actually see.
←Artemisia Gentileschi Huang Gongwang→
